info.title: Retino API
info.description:
# Retino API v2

The Retino API lets you integrate your e-commerce operations with Retino's platform. Sync orders from your store, manage customer service tickets, track shipments across carriers, and automate your workflow.

**Base URL:** `https://app.retino.com/api/v2/`

## Quick Start

All API requests require authentication using your API token. Add it to the `Authorization` header:

```http
GET /api/v2/orders HTTP/1.1
Host: app.retino.com
Authorization: abc123def456...
```

**Get your API token:** https://app.retino.com/settings/api-v2/api/

## API Characteristics

- **REST architecture** with standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE)
- **JSON format** for all requests and responses
- **Idempotent operations** for safe order and ticket creation (using your unique identifiers)
- **Standard HTTP status codes** (200, 201, 400, 404, etc.)
- **Field-level validation** with detailed error messages

## What You Can Do

### Orders
Send orders from your e-commerce system to Retino. Each order is identified by your store ID and order number. Use the same endpoint to create new orders and update existing ones.

### Tickets
Create and manage customer service cases (returns, warranty claims, inquiries). Track products, refunds, shipping status, and communication history.

### Tracking
Track shipments from carriers worldwide. Send a tracking number and Retino automatically fetches delivery status updates. Search and monitor all shipments in one place.

### Shipping Portal
Manage shipping workflow and form progress for your shipping operations.

## Error Responses

The API returns structured errors with HTTP status codes and detailed messages:

```json
{
  "detail": "Order not found",
}
```

For validation errors, you'll receive field-specific feedback:

```json
{
  "customer_email": ["Enter a valid email address."],
  "total_price": ["Ensure this value has no more than 2 decimal places."]
}
```

## Endpoint Organization

Endpoints are organized into categories in the navigation:

- **Core** - User management
- **Orders** - Order and document management
- **Tickets** - Customer service operations
- **Tracking** - Shipment tracking
- **Shipping Portal** - Shipping workflow

## Support

Questions? Contact us at support@retino.com or visit https://docs.retino.com

---

post.description:
Send an order from your system to Retino. This endpoint automatically creates new orders or updates existing ones - you don't need separate endpoints for create vs update.

**How to use:**
1. Send your order with `code` (your order number) and `store_id` (your store identifier)
2. If this `code` + `store_id` combination doesn't exist yet → new order created (HTTP 201)
3. If this `code` + `store_id` already exists → order updated with your new data (HTTP 200)

**Integration tips:**
- Use the same `store_id` value for all orders from the same store/shop
- Send the complete order data each time - we'll update what changed
- Include items in the same request to avoid extra API calls
- The `store_id` is permanent and cannot be changed after order creation

To manage store ids in your account, go to [Settings → API](https://app.retino.io/settings/api-v2/).

**Initial Import Mode:**
Add `?is_initial_import=true` query parameter to skip automation triggers for this order (emails, etc.). Use this when importing historical orders to avoid sending outdated notifications to customers.

parameters.name: is_initial_import
parameters.description: Set to `true` to skip automation triggers for orders from initial import.

options.description: Mixin that automatically adds is_initial_import flag to serializer context based on query parameter.

patch.description:
Update the order's status (e.g., mark as paid, shipped, or cancelled). The status change is logged with a timestamp in the order history.

**Example:** `PATCH /api/v2/orders/{id}/status/?status_value=PAID&external_name=Zaplaceno`

parameters.name: external_name
parameters.description: Status name as used in the online store system. Used to create status mapping records.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Order UUID

parameters.name: status_value
parameters.description: New status value. Must be one of the supported status values.

schema.error: Invalid status value or unable to apply status

schema.detail: No Order matches the given query.

post.description:
Send multiple orders in a single request - useful for syncing your entire order catalog or processing batches. Each order works the same as the single order endpoint (create if new, update if exists).

**Batch size limit: Maximum 50 orders per request.**

**Rate limit: 60 requests per minute per API key.**

For larger imports, split your data into batches of 50 orders each. Returns HTTP 200 if all succeed, 207 if some fail, or 400 if all fail or batch size exceeds limit.

**Initial Import Mode:**
Add `?is_initial_import=true` query parameter to skip automation triggers for these orders (emails, etc.). Use this when importing historical orders to avoid sending outdated notifications to customers.

parameters.name: is_initial_import
parameters.description: Set to `true` to skip automation triggers for orders from initial import.

schema.description: Partial success - some orders processed, some failed
All orders failed validation or batch size exceeds 50 orders

error.example: Bulk operations are limited to 50 orders. You sent 51 orders. Please split your request into smaller batches.

get.description:
List all tickets with optional filtering.

**Filtering options:**
- `created_at_from` - Show tickets created after this date
- `updated_at_from` - Show tickets updated after this date
- `closed_at_from` - Show tickets closed after this date
- `is_closed` - Filter by open/closed status

Results include full ticket details, history, products, and refund information.

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: Order ticket list by field. Prepended - to the field name indicates that the ordering is descending.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

post.description:
Create a customer service ticket (return, warranty claim, inquiry).

**Required fields:**
- `customer` - Customer contact details
- `type` - Ticket type ID ([get available types from /ticket-types](#tag/ticket-resources/get/api/v2/ticket-types))
- `state` - Initial state ID (get available states from /ticket-states)
- `language` - Customer language
- `currency` - Ticket currency
- `country` - Customer country

**Optional but useful:**
- `order_id` - Link to related order
- `owner` - Assign to a team member
- `tags` - Categorize the ticket

get.description: Retrieve complete details of a single ticket, including all history items, products, shipping info, and refund requests.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this ticket.

put.description: Replace all ticket fields with new values. Usually you'll want to use PATCH for partial updates instead.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A UUID string identifying this ticket.

patch.description:
Update only the fields you send, leaving others unchanged.

**Common updates:**
- Change state: `{"state": "<state_id>"}`
- Assign owner: `{"owner": "<user_id>"}`
- Add tags: `{"tags": ["<tag_id>", "<tag_id>"]}`
- Update language: `{"language": "de"}`

get.description:
List all your tracked shipments with current delivery status.

**Filtering options:**
- `created_at_from` - Shipments added after this date
- `updated_at_from` - Shipments with status updates after this date
- `ordered_at_from` - Shipments ordered after this date
- `delivered_at_from` - Shipments delivered after this date
- `carrier` - Filter by carrier name (can specify multiple)
- `status` - Filter by delivery status (can specify multiple)
- `tag` - Filter by tag ID (can specify multiple)
- `tracking_number` - Filter by tracking number (case-insensitive partial match)
- `order_code` - Filter by order code (case-insensitive partial match)
- `page_size` - Results per page (default 50)

Results include full tracking history and current status for each shipment.

post.description:
Start tracking a shipment by providing its tracking number.

**How it works:**
1. Send the tracking number and optionally a carrier hint
2. Retino automatically detects the carrier and starts tracking
3. Status updates arrive automatically as the package moves

**Required field:**
- `tracking_number` - The tracking number from the carrier

**Recommended:**
- `carrier_hint` - Carrier name if you know it (helps with detection)
- `delivery_type` - TO_ADDRESS or TO_BRANCH

**Optional:**
- `order_code` - Link to your order number
- `weight` and `weight_unit` - Package weight info
- `parcel_shop` - Pickup location details for branch/locker delivery

get.description: Get detailed tracking information for a single shipment, including full delivery history and current status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Shipment ID

name.description: Status name (see order status enums for valid values)

external_name.description: Status name as used in the online store system. Used to create status mapping records.

date.description: Timestamp when this status was set

items.description: List of time periods for this day

opening_hours.description: Weekly opening hours as array of 7 days (Monday-Sunday). Each day contains array of time periods. Example: [[['07:30', '21:00']], [['07:30', '21:00']], ...]

code.description: Unique code identifying the parcel shop

is_parcel_locker.description: Indicates whether the place is a parcel locker

opening_hours.description: Array of 7 days (Monday to Sunday), each day containing array of time intervals. Each time interval is an array with exactly 2 elements: [start_time, end_time]. Empty array [] means the shop is closed that day. Example: [[['06:00', '17:00']], [['06:00', '17:00']], [['06:00', '16:00']], [['06:00', '16:00']], [['06:00', '17:00']], [['07:00', '11:00']], []]

external_tracking_number.description: Tracking number of the second carrier if the shipping is mediated by another carrier

id.description: Unique primary identifier of the shipping

last_sync_at.description: Date of the last status synchronization with the carrier

order_id.description: ID of the associated order. [Create/update orders](#tag/orders/post/api/v2/orders/)

original_tracking_number.description: Regular Tracking number can be changed when the shipping is mediated by another carrier. (Deprecated, use tracking_number and external_tracking_number)

parcel_count.description: Number of parcels in the shipment

picked_from_branch_at.description: Date of the pickup from the branch by recipient

pickup_at.description: Date of the pickup from the sender

shipment_type.description: Type of the shipment, e.g. 'standard', 'express', etc.

source.description: Source from where the shipping was imported

stored_until.description: Date until the shipment is stored for personal pickup

tags.title: Shipping tags
tags.description: List of shipping tag IDs. [List tracking tags](#tag/tracking-tags/get/api/v2/tracking-tags/)

TransportStatusEnum.description:
* `STATUS_PENDING_CONFIRMATION` - Čeká na potvrzení
* `STATUS_INIT` - Povolena
* `STATUS_ACCEPTED` - Vytvořen štítek
* `STATUS_SUBMITTED` - Na pobočce
* `STATUS_TRANSPORT` - Přepravováno
* `STATUS_DELIVERED` - Doručeno
* `STATUS_UNDELIVERED` - Nedoručeno
* `STATUS_CANCELED` - Zrušená
* `STATUS_WAITING_FOR_ORDERING` - Status Waiting For Ordering
* `STATUS_REFUNDED` - Refundováno
* `STATUS_UNKNOWN` - Neznámý

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Get team members in your account. Use these user IDs when assigning tickets.

tags.name: Files
tags.description: Upload files and get file IDs. Use these file IDs when creating order documents or attaching files to other resources.

tags.name: Orders
tags.description: Send orders from your e-commerce system to Retino. Each order is identified by your order number (code) and store ID. Use the same endpoint to both create new orders and update existing ones.

tags.name: Order Documents
tags.description: Attach invoices, receipts, and shipping labels to orders.

tags.name: Tickets
tags.description: Create and manage customer service tickets (returns, warranty claims, inquiries). Track products, refunds, shipping, and communication history for each case.

tags.name: Products
tags.description: Manage products within tickets - track items involved in returns, warranty claims, or other customer service cases.

tags.name: Ticket Resources
tags.description: Get available ticket states, tags, types, and custom fields for your account. Use these to configure and categorize your tickets correctly.

tags.name: Form Progress
tags.description: Manage form progress and shipping portal workflow.

tags.name: Shipments
tags.description: Track shipments from carriers worldwide. Send tracking numbers and Retino will automatically fetch delivery status updates. Search and monitor all your shipments in one place.

tags.name: Tracking Tags
tags.description: Get available tags for categorizing shipments (priority, fragile, etc.).

tags.name: Tracking Issues
tags.description: Get available tracking issue types (delivery problems, delays, lost packages).

tags.name: Store Branches
tags.description: Manage your store branches (pickup locations). These can be displayed in tracking emails as delivery destinations.
